title: WASM standalone RefStore: wire MemoryRefStore as default in WASM build
description:
Make WASM document ops work with no host bridge by defaulting to MemoryRefStore in src/wasm/root.zig and making ImportedRefStore opt-in.

Why:
- After MemoryRefStore lands, WASM should not require a JS hostBridge to demo document put/get/list/history/delete.
- Keep ImportedRefStore available for hosts that want to persist beyond the WASM instance (IndexedDB story).

Scope:
- Replace the unconditional ImportedRefStore wiring with a runtime/init-time selector. Default backend = MemoryRefStore. Selector exposed to host (e.g. opts in loadSideshowdbClient or a sideshowdb_set_backend export) so hosts can switch to ImportedRefStore explicitly.
- TypeScript bindings: hostBridge becomes optional and the docs update accordingly. When omitted, client uses in-WASM memory store. host-bridge error path only fires when the user opted into ImportedRefStore.
- Update bindings/typescript/sideshowdb-core tests: cover no-bridge happy path including put/get/list/history/delete round trip.
- Update README playground/quick-start to lead with the no-bridge example.

EARS:
- When loadSideshowdbClient is called without hostBridge, the client shall default to the WASM in-process MemoryRefStore.
- Where hostBridge is provided, the client shall route ref operations through the host bridge instead of MemoryRefStore.
- If hostBridge is provided but a ref op throws, then the client shall return a host-bridge error result without mutating MemoryRefStore state.

Acceptance:
- 'zig build wasm' produces an artifact whose document_put/get/list/history/delete work end-to-end with no JS imports beyond memory ones.
- TypeScript acceptance suite covers no-bridge round-trip.
- README quick-start example does not pass hostBridge.
close_reason: WASM defaults to MemoryRefStore via runtime selector (commit 15db230). hostBridge optional; loadSideshowdbClient flips backend when bridge is supplied

title: WASM standalone RefStore: in-memory backend (Option A)
description:
Add freestanding-safe MemoryRefStore in src/core so WASM can run document ops without a host bridge.

Why:
- src/wasm/root.zig only wires ImportedRefStore today, so any WASM consumer must implement a JS host bridge.
- src/core/storage.zig gates ZiggitRefStore to non-freestanding (filesystem-bound), so ziggit work did not give WASM standalone capability.
- MVP playground/demo story needs WASM to just work after loadSideshowdbClient with no host wiring.

Scope:
- Implement MemoryRefStore with full RefStore vtable (put/get/delete/list/history) using only std + allocator (no FS, no process, no extern). Append-only version history per key.
- Compiles for wasm32-freestanding. Native build also exposes it (useful for tests).
- Unit tests covering happy/negative/edge/boundary per CLAUDE.md TDD bar (empty key, empty value, missing key get, version-pinned get, history ordering, delete-then-list, overwrite preserves prior version).
- EARS in description.

EARS:
- The MemoryRefStore shall return a unique VersionId for each successful put.
- When get is called with a known key and no version, the MemoryRefStore shall return the most recently put value.
- When get is called with a key that has no entries, the MemoryRefStore shall return null.
- When get is called with a version that does not exist for the key, the MemoryRefStore shall return null.
- When delete is called for a known key, the MemoryRefStore shall remove the key from list output and from future no-version get calls.
- The MemoryRefStore shall preserve historical versions of a key after subsequent puts so history returns them in put order.
- If the store is dropped, then all data shall be lost (volatile by design, documented).

Acceptance:
- 'zig build test' green on native including MemoryRefStore unit tests.
- 'zig build wasm' compiles MemoryRefStore into wasm32-freestanding artifact.
- No new dependencies.
close_reason: MemoryRefStore landed in mvp-wasm-standalone branch (commit 19d731c)

title: Fix clean-run JS acceptance build prerequisites
description: CI exposed that the root build:acceptance script compiles acceptance/typescript before @sideshowdb/core and @sideshowdb/effect have been built in a clean checkout. The acceptance verifier also allows this to slip through because it runs bun run acceptance before zig build js:acceptance, leaving binding outputs in place. Update the root acceptance build path so it bootstraps binding outputs, and strengthen the verifier so both bun run acceptance and zig build js:acceptance are exercised from wiped outputs.
acceptance_criteria: When a clean checkout runs bun run build:acceptance, the workspace shall build @sideshowdb/core and @sideshowdb/effect before compiling acceptance/typescript. When scripts/verify-js-acceptance.sh runs, the verifier shall wipe generated artifacts before each public acceptance entrypoint so missing prerequisites fail locally. If the TypeScript acceptance workspace depends on built package outputs, then the repo root acceptance build path shall produce those outputs before invoking tsc for the acceptance workspace.
close_reason: Completed

title: Document CLI commands and options
description:
Add a dedicated CLI reference to the public docs and link it from the README/getting-started docs.

EARS:
- The Sideshowdb docs shall provide a CLI Reference page that documents the supported top-level usage, global options, commands, subcommands, and command-specific options.
- The CLI Reference page shall include examples for version, doc put, doc get, doc list, doc delete, and doc history.
- The CLI Reference page shall document backend selection precedence for --refstore, SIDESHOWDB_REFSTORE, .sideshowdb/config.toml, and the built-in default.
- The CLI Reference page shall document --data-file behavior, including file-over-stdin precedence and missing-file failure behavior.
- The README shall link readers to the full CLI Reference instead of carrying the full command catalog inline.
acceptance_criteria: site/src/routes/docs/cli/+page.md exists with all current CLI commands and options documented. README and Getting Started link to /docs/cli/. Site check/build gates pass.
close_reason: Added CLI reference docs, README/Getting Started links, docs EARS, and site tests.

title: Browser persistence + sync: IndexedDB-backed RefStore
description:
Persist browser WASM RefStore state across reloads with an IndexedDB-backed default host bridge in @sideshowdb/core, while preserving the existing host-bridge override seam for advanced integrations.

Why:
- MemoryRefStore in WASM is volatile; browser users otherwise lose state on reload.
- The architecture spec treats browser IndexedDB as a disposable local materialization layer and natural persistence target.
- We need deterministic backend selection before remote sync lands so browser behavior is predictable.

Scope:
- Implement a default IndexedDB-backed RefStore host bridge in @sideshowdb/core.
- Backend selection precedence for browser runtime:
  1) Explicit hostBridge option (caller-provided) wins.
  2) Otherwise, IndexedDB default bridge when indexedDB is available.
  3) Otherwise, fallback to in-WASM MemoryRefStore.
- Define IndexedDB schema contract for Option A:
  - Object store keyed by RefStore key.
  - Value shape stores append-only version history entries [{ version, value }] per key.
- On client boot, hydrate in-memory bridge cache from IndexedDB before first document operation.
- On put/delete, serialize persistence writes to preserve operation order and avoid out-of-order durable state.
- Expose bridge constructor for advanced callers while keeping browser default automatic.

Out of scope:
- Remote push/pull protocol and conflict resolution semantics (tracked in sideshowdb-ig3).
- Ziggit virtual-FS parity layout work (tracked in sideshowdb-dgz).

EARS:
- When loadSideshowdbClient is called in a browser-like runtime without a hostBridge and indexedDB is available, the Sideshowdb TypeScript client shall route RefStore operations through a default IndexedDB-backed host bridge.
- When loadSideshowdbClient is called with an explicit hostBridge, the Sideshowdb TypeScript client shall use the explicit hostBridge instead of the default IndexedDB bridge.
- If indexedDB is unavailable and no explicit hostBridge is provided, then the Sideshowdb TypeScript client shall continue using the in-WASM MemoryRefStore.
- When the IndexedDB-backed bridge starts, it shall hydrate key/version history state from IndexedDB before serving RefStore reads.
- When consecutive puts target the same key, the IndexedDB-backed bridge shall persist the resulting versions in operation order.
- When a page reload occurs after at least one successful put through the default IndexedDB bridge, subsequent gets shall return the persisted value for that key.
acceptance_criteria:
- Browser default persistence: in browser-like tests with IndexedDB available and no hostBridge provided, a put in one client instance is readable from a new client instance after reload simulation.
- Precedence: when hostBridge is provided, client routes through hostBridge and does not rely on default IndexedDB behavior.
- Fallback: when IndexedDB is unavailable and no hostBridge is provided, document ops continue to succeed via in-WASM MemoryRefStore semantics.
- Ordering: sequential puts persist in deterministic order, and history/get reflect the latest write after reload.
- Public API/docs: @sideshowdb/core docs describe IndexedDB as browser default and document opt-out/fallback behavior.
notes:
IndexedDB hardening + Effect bindings follow-up:
- Bridge now upgrades missing storeName by bumping DB version and auto-closing on versionchange.
- Added dedicated indexeddb-bridge tests.
- Added @sideshowdb/effect helper createIndexedDbRefHostBridgeEffect using Effect monadic composition and chaining.
- Adjusted core precedence test expectation for hostBridge key shape.

title: Remote sync: push/pull RefStore to a remote git endpoint
description:
Sync the local RefStore to a remote git repository so multiple sideshowdb clients (CLI and WASM) can converge on shared state.

Why:
- MVP value prop is event-sourced + offline-friendly + Git-backed; without a sync path the Git backing is invisible to users.
- Unblocks collaborative scenarios for both native (server) and browser (peer) clients.

Scope:
- Define sync semantics: which ref(s) are exchanged, conflict policy (fast-forward only vs merge of append-only history), authentication surface (https token, ssh).
- Add 'sideshowdb sync push <remote>' and 'sideshowdb sync pull <remote>' CLI commands.
- Native impl uses ziggit (preferred) or git subprocess fallback.
- Document sync behavior in README and docs/development/specs.
- EARS-driven design doc + acceptance tests (CLI cucumber + integration).

Out of scope here: IndexedDB-backed sync (separate issue), conflict-free CRDT layer.

EARS (initial draft):
- When 'sync push' is called against a configured remote, the CLI shall upload the local RefStore ref so a fresh clone receives the same document state.
- When 'sync pull' is called against a configured remote, the CLI shall fetch the remote ref and fast-forward the local RefStore.
- If the remote ref has diverged from the local ref, then the CLI shall fail with a clear non-zero error and leave local state unchanged.
- Where authentication is required, the CLI shall accept credentials via standard git mechanisms (SSH agent, credential helper, env tokens).

Acceptance:
- Roundtrip: client A puts doc, sync push; client B sync pull, get returns same doc + version history.
- Divergence test fails predictably.
- README and a dedicated 'docs/development/specs/sync-spec.md' describe the contract.

title: Add --data-file option to doc put
description:
Add CLI support for 'sideshowdb doc put' to accept '--data-file <path>' so payload body can come from a file instead of stdin.

EARS:
- When 'doc put' is invoked with '--data-file <path>', the CLI shall read payload bytes from that file and use them as the stored payload body.
- If '--data-file' points to a missing or unreadable file, then the CLI shall fail with a non-zero exit code and a clear error message without mutating document state.
- If both stdin payload input and '--data-file' are present, then the CLI shall apply one deterministic precedence rule and document it.
- When '--data-file' is used with '--json', the CLI shall emit the same envelope success shape as stdin-based 'doc put'.

Acceptance:
- 'sideshowdb --json doc put --type note --id file-demo --data-file payload.json' stores payload.json data.
- Missing/unreadable '--data-file' fails non-zero and does not create/update the target document.
- Tests cover happy path, missing-file failure, and stdin-vs-file precedence behavior.
- CLI docs/README include '--data-file' usage and precedence notes.
close_reason: --data-file landed in mvp-wasm-standalone (commit 18fd090); README documents precedence

title: Add --data-file support for doc put payload input
description:
Add CLI support for 'sideshowdb doc put' to accept a '--data-file <path>' option that reads the document payload body from a file instead of stdin. This should make scripted and large-payload workflows easier and less error-prone while preserving existing stdin-based behavior.

EARS:
- When 'doc put' is invoked with '--data-file <path>', the CLI shall read payload bytes from the referenced file and use them as the payload body for document storage.
- If '--data-file' is provided and the file does not exist or cannot be read, then the CLI shall fail with a non-zero exit code and a clear error message without mutating document state.
- If both stdin payload input and '--data-file' are provided, then the CLI shall use a single deterministic precedence rule and document that behavior.
- When '--data-file' is used with '--json', the CLI shall emit the same successful envelope output shape as stdin-based 'doc put'.

Acceptance:
- 'sideshowdb --json doc put --type note --id file-demo --data-file payload.json' succeeds and stores the payload from payload.json.
- Missing/unreadable '--data-file' path returns non-zero and does not create/update the document ref.
- Test coverage includes happy path, missing-file failure path, and precedence behavior when stdin is also present.
- README/docs include an example of '--data-file' usage and any precedence notes.

title: Fix @sideshowdb/core ESM runtime imports for Node consumers
description:
The public @sideshowdb/core package root cannot be value-imported by the TypeScript acceptance runtime under cucumber-js/Node because bindings/typescript/sideshowdb-core/dist/index.js re-exports ./types without a .js extension. Type-only imports compile away, but the new direct WASM acceptance loader triggers the first runtime import and fails before scenarios execute.

EARS:
- When a Node ESM consumer imports the public @sideshowdb/core package root at runtime, the package shall resolve without module-not-found errors.
- If the public package root re-exports built files, then the emitted runtime import specifiers shall include extensions required by the target module loader.

Acceptance:
- bun run --cwd acceptance/typescript acceptance:raw -- --tags "@wasm" reaches scenario execution after build:bindings
- bun run --cwd acceptance/typescript acceptance:raw runs without ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND from @sideshowdb/core/dist/index.js
close_reason: Completed within sideshowdb-28n by emitting .js ESM specifiers from @sideshowdb/core source files.

title: Fix TypeScript acceptance:raw support-code imports
description:
The acceptance/typescript Cucumber runner currently executes feature files without importing dist support code when invoked via 'bun run --cwd acceptance/typescript acceptance:raw -- --tags @wasm'. The WASM and CLI step files compile and execute when passed explicitly through --import, but the default script path leaves every step undefined.

Acceptance:
- Running 'bun run --cwd acceptance/typescript acceptance:raw -- --tags @wasm' imports the compiled support and step modules without extra CLI flags
- The acceptance:raw script exercises compiled dist support code consistently for CLI and WASM slices
- The WASM slice passes through the standard acceptance:raw command once imports are wired correctly
close_reason: Completed within sideshowdb-28n via the acceptance/typescript/cucumber.js runner config fix.

title: Fix clean-checkout JS test prerequisites for WASM bindings
description:
The root Bun workspace verification path is not clean-checkout safe. In a fresh worktree, both `bun run test` and `zig build js:test` fail before exercising the TypeScript bindings because the workspace assumes prebuilt artifacts already exist. The core binding tests read `zig-out/wasm/sideshowdb.wasm`, and the Effect package resolves `@sideshowdb/core` through its built `dist` export, so a fresh checkout needs both the WASM artifact and built binding outputs before root JS tests can pass.

EARS:
- When `zig build js:test` runs in a clean checkout, the repo shall build any WASM and binding-package artifacts required by the JS workspace tests before running them.
- When the root Bun workspace test command runs in a clean checkout, the TypeScript binding test suites shall not fail with missing `zig-out/wasm/sideshowdb.wasm` or unresolved workspace package exports.
- If JS workspace tests depend on generated artifacts, then the repo shall either generate those artifacts automatically or load test fixtures that do not require manual prebuild steps.

Acceptance:
- In a fresh worktree with no preexisting `zig-out/wasm/sideshowdb.wasm` and no binding `dist/` outputs, `zig build js:test` passes without any manual prerequisite commands.
- The root verification path documents or encodes the correct prerequisite order instead of relying on stale local build outputs.
- The fix preserves build.zig as the top-level orchestrator for the JS test workflow.
close_reason: Encoded clean-checkout-safe JS workspace prerequisites in root check/test scripts and wired zig build js:test to explicit wasm + binding build dependencies; verified with bash scripts/verify-js-workspace.sh and zig build js:check after deleting binding dist outputs and the wasm artifact.

title: CLI stdout writer overwrites prior file content when redirected
description: When the sideshowdb CLI is invoked multiple times with stdout redirected to the same regular file via shell { ... } > file aggregation, each invocation appears to seek to offset 0 and overwrite prior content rather than appending to the inherited file position. Repro: { echo '{...}' | sideshowdb --json doc put ... ; sideshowdb --json doc get ... ; sideshowdb --json doc delete ... ; sideshowdb --json doc list ... ; } > /tmp/log 2>&1 — the resulting file ends up only ~155 bytes instead of the expected ~490+ bytes. Workaround: pipe each invocation through cat. Likely cause: src/cli/main.zig std.Io.File.Writer.init(.stdout(), ...) initializing a fresh writer that resets position 0 when stdout is a regular file.
acceptance_criteria: CLI binary preserves shell-inherited stdout position; chained invocations into one redirect concatenate cleanly without trailing overwrite; regression test exercising { put; get; delete; list; } > file flow.
notes: Fix submitted in PR #16 (https://github.com/sideshowdb/sideshowdb/pull/16). Awaiting review/merge before closing.
close_reason: Merged via PR #16

title: Document ziggit default refstore and run final regression
description: Update docs/development/specs/git-ref-storage-spec.md with ziggit-default + subprocess-fallback + selector precedence. Run zig fmt --check, zig build (ReleaseSafe), check:core-docs, wasm, test. Run JS/site gates: js:build-bindings, js:check, js:test, verify-site-build-from-clean-bindings.sh, verify-site-reference-build.sh. Open PR. Step 7 of plan.
acceptance_criteria: spec updated; all zig + JS + site gates exit 0; PR opened referencing sideshowdb-cnm and sideshowdb-w1i.
close_reason: Spec updated; fmt normalized; 47/47 tests pass on Debug + ReleaseSafe; JS gates + site verify scripts pass.

title: Resolve refstore from env and .sideshowdb/config.toml
description: Implement fromEnvironment (SIDESHOWDB_REFSTORE), minimal TOML fromConfig ([storage] refstore), and resolve() with precedence: flag > env > config > default ziggit. Add precedence tests including env-overridden-by-flag and invalid-env-fails-without-flag. Step 6 of plan.
acceptance_criteria: precedence flag>env>config>default; invalid env fails clearly; tests pass; README documents precedence.
close_reason: Selector resolves flag>env>config>default; env-overrides-config and config-when-absent tests pass; 47/47 tests pass.

title: Add CLI --refstore backend selector flag
description: Create src/cli/refstore_selector.zig with RefStoreBackend enum and Selection types. Parse global --refstore in src/cli/app.zig. Wire backend choice into runDocumentCommand switch. Add CLI tests for flag selection and invalid-backend failure without mutation. Step 5 of plan.
acceptance_criteria: --refstore ziggit|subprocess works; bogus backend fails before mutating refs; tests pass.
close_reason: CLI --refstore flag parses ziggit/subprocess; bogus selector fails before mutation; 43/43 tests pass.

title: Make ZiggitRefStore the native default GitRefStore
description: Change pub const GitRefStore = SubprocessGitRefStore to GitRefStore = ZiggitRefStore in src/core/storage.zig. Add type-name equality test. SubprocessGitRefStore stays exported. Step 4 of plan.
acceptance_criteria: GitRefStore equals ZiggitRefStore on native targets; existing CLI/document tests pass on ziggit.
close_reason: GitRefStore aliased to ZiggitRefStore on native; subprocess callers migrated to SubprocessGitRefStore explicitly; 41/41 tests pass.

title: Add ZiggitRefStore backend under production names
description: Add ziggit dependency in build.zig.zon (or vendor compatibility subset). Port src/core/storage/ziggit_ref_store.zig from exploration branch. Export ZiggitRefStore via storage.zig and root.zig. Add tests/ziggit_ref_store_test.zig with parity + metacharacter tests. Step 3 of plan.
acceptance_criteria: ZiggitRefStore exported; parity test passes; metacharacter literal-key test passes; no ziggit object IDs leak from impl file.
close_reason: ZiggitRefStore ported from exploration with vendored ziggit_pkg shim; parity + metacharacter tests pass (40/40).

title: Rename subprocess refstore without changing default
description: Rename GitRefStore impl to SubprocessGitRefStore in src/core/storage/git_ref_store.zig. Export SubprocessGitRefStore via storage.zig and root.zig. Keep GitRefStore aliased to SubprocessGitRefStore (no default change yet). Step 2 of plan.
acceptance_criteria: SubprocessGitRefStore exported; GitRefStore still aliases subprocess; tests pass.
close_reason: SubprocessGitRefStore exported; GitRefStore alias preserved; tests pass.

title: Extract shared RefStore parity harness
description: Create tests/ref_store_parity.zig with shared exerciseRefStore harness. Refactor tests/git_ref_store_test.zig to use it. Behavior must stay green (refactor only). Step 1 of plan docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-28-ziggit-default-refstore-backend-implementation.md.
acceptance_criteria: tests/ref_store_parity.zig exists with exerciseRefStore; tests/git_ref_store_test.zig delegates to harness; zig build test passes.
close_reason: Shared parity harness extracted; subprocess test refactored to delegate; 37/37 tests pass.

title: Make ziggit the default selectable RefStore backend
description:
Promote the ziggit-backed RefStore work from the closed exploration branch into the production storage path. The native ziggit backend should become the default non-freestanding GitRefStore while the existing subprocess implementation remains available as an explicitly selectable fallback for compatibility and debugging. CLI users must be able to select the backend via command-line flag, environment variable, and repo-local config, with clear precedence and clear failures for invalid backend names.

EARS:
- The Sideshowdb native storage layer shall default to a ziggit-backed GitRefStore on non-freestanding targets.
- The Sideshowdb native storage layer shall keep the subprocess-backed Git implementation available as an explicit fallback backend.
- When a CLI user passes --refstore ziggit or --refstore subprocess, the Sideshowdb CLI shall use the requested backend for document operations.
- When no --refstore flag is passed and SIDESHOWDB_REFSTORE is set, the Sideshowdb CLI shall use the backend named by that environment variable.
- When no --refstore flag or environment override is provided and .sideshowdb/config.toml contains [storage] refstore, the Sideshowdb CLI shall use the configured backend.
- The Sideshowdb CLI shall resolve backend selection in this precedence order: command-line flag, environment variable, config file, built-in default.
- If a backend selector names an unsupported backend, then the Sideshowdb CLI shall fail with a clear usage/configuration error and shall not mutate document refs.
- The ziggit-backed backend shall preserve the existing RefStore put, get, delete, list, and history behavior, including commit-SHA VersionId values.
- The subprocess-backed fallback shall remain covered by the same RefStore parity suite as the ziggit-backed backend.

Acceptance:
- GitRefStore resolves to the ziggit-backed backend by default on native targets.
- SubprocessGitRefStore remains available and tested.
- CLI backend selection supports --refstore, SIDESHOWDB_REFSTORE, and .sideshowdb/config.toml with documented precedence.
- Shared parity tests pass for ziggit and subprocess backends.
- Existing document, transport, CLI, WASM, and TypeScript verification remains green.
acceptance_criteria: Default native backend is ziggit; subprocess fallback is selectable; CLI flag/env/config precedence is tested; invalid backend names fail without mutation; parity tests cover both backends; full repo quality gates pass.
close_reason: All 7 implementation tasks complete; ZiggitRefStore default; SubprocessGitRefStore selectable via flag/env/config; parity tests pass; full regression green.

title: Fix /reference/api 404 during site build
description:
zig build site:build succeeds, but the SvelteKit build logs [404] GET /reference/api/ while assembling the static site. Track down whether the generated Zig autodoc asset is missing at build time, linked incorrectly, or fetched before staging completes.

EARS:
- When the docs site builds the reference section, the Sideshowdb site shall resolve /reference/api/ without emitting a 404 during the static build.
- If generated reference docs are unavailable, then the Sideshowdb site shall fail the build with a clear error or hide the broken link path instead of logging a runtime 404.

Acceptance:
- zig build site:build completes without a /reference/api/ 404 log
- The reference docs route still serves the generated Zig autodoc output in the built site
close_reason: Resolved reference API prerender 404 by linking docs and iframe to the generated static index asset and failing other prerender HTTP errors.

title: Remove duplicate H1 from docs pages
description: Each docs page renders title twice — sveltepress frontmatter title renders H1, and the markdown body also has # Title. Drop the markdown H1 since frontmatter handles it.
close_reason: Closed

title: Fix dark/light theme contrast on docs site
description: Site (sveltepress) inherits bg overlays from app.css that sit above docs content (theme-default--page-layout has no z-index). Forces color-scheme: light without dark token overrides; sveltepress dark mode (html.dark) leaves atlas tokens locked at light values, producing low-contrast text in dark mode and overlays obscuring text in both modes.
close_reason: Closed

title: Clean up Getting Started page (EARS bleed + prereq scope)
description: Getting Started rendered page contains an internal 'Implements EARS' blockquote that should live in a spec file, not in user-facing docs. Prerequisites table currently applies to all install paths but is really only needed for source-build. Move EARS to docs/development/specs/, restructure prerequisites under From source, simplify release-binary path.
close_reason: Closed

title: Switch mise install docs from ubi to github backend
description: ubi backend is being deprecated in mise; switch documented install commands to mise github backend in README and Getting Started.
close_reason: Closed

title: Align install docs across README and Getting Started
description: Getting Started page only documents source build; README documents release-binary install (mise/ubi/direct download). Align both to cover release install + source build with EARS coverage.
close_reason: Closed

title: zig build siteDev fails when site/node_modules missing
description:
zig build siteDev (and siteOnly, sitePreview) shells into site/ and runs bun run <script>, but node_modules is never installed by the build graph. Fresh checkouts and CI (when not pre-installed) hit 'vite: command not found' from bun.

Repro:
- rm -rf site/node_modules
- zig build siteDev
- => '/bin/bash: vite: command not found' exit 127

EARS:
- Where the site Bun workspace is missing node_modules, the siteDev/siteOnly/sitePreview build steps shall install dependencies before invoking bun run scripts.
- When site/bun.lock is unchanged since the last successful install, the install step shall be a fast no-op.

Acceptance:
- New zig build siteInstall step that runs 'bun install' inside site/.
- siteDev, siteOnly, sitePreview each depend on siteInstall.
- 'rm -rf site/node_modules && zig build siteDev' produces a working dev server without manual bun install.
acceptance_criteria: Fresh clone or wiped node_modules: 'zig build siteDev' starts the dev server without manual bun install. Steps that run bun scripts depend on siteInstall.
close_reason: Implemented in PR #8 (squash 0cdce0a). siteInstall step wired into siteDev/siteOnly/sitePreview.

title: Add site quality gates: link check, a11y, lighthouse, search
description:
Site lacks automated quality gates. No link checker so reference cross-links can rot. No accessibility check so theme regressions go unnoticed. No search so docs are unbrowseable as content grows. No performance budget despite open chunk-size issue (sideshowdb-ivp).

EARS:
- The Sideshowdb site CI shall fail if any internal link in the assembled Pages artifact is broken.
- The Sideshowdb site CI shall fail if axe-core (or equivalent) reports any serious or critical accessibility violation on Home, Docs, Playground, Reference.
- The Sideshowdb site CI shall record a Lighthouse score for the homepage and shall fail when Performance, Accessibility, or Best Practices fall below a configured threshold.
- The Sideshowdb site shall provide full-text search across authored docs (Pagefind or equivalent static index).
- Where reference docs link to authored concepts, the link checker shall validate both directions.

Acceptance:
- New CI step runs link checker against dist/pages and blocks on broken links
- New CI step runs axe + lighthouse against built site
- Pagefind (or equivalent) integrated, searchable in browser, indexed at build time
- Thresholds documented in scripts/ or workflow
acceptance_criteria: CI runs link check + axe + lighthouse against assembled artifact with documented thresholds. Pagefind index built and search UI live. Broken links / a11y violations / score regressions fail CI.

title: Wire WASM into playground tour mode that narrates Git→Sideshowdb mapping
description:
Current playground fetches refs and shows a static blurb. WASM module is loaded but never consumes fetched data. Spec requires guided inspection through small set of high-value views with explanatory copy that maps GitHub/Git data to Sideshowdb concepts. Playground today does not deliver that.

EARS:
- When a user loads a repo in the playground, the Sideshowdb site shall pass fetched ref data to the loaded WASM module for projection.
- When the WASM module returns a projection, the Sideshowdb site shall render at least one Sideshowdb-derived view alongside the raw GitHub view.
- The Sideshowdb playground shall present a stepwise tour with at least three narration steps mapping repository structures to Sideshowdb concepts.
- If the WASM module fails to load or projection fails, then the Sideshowdb site shall continue to render the source data view and shall explain that derived views are unavailable.

Acceptance:
- Playground calls a WASM-exported projection function on real fetched ref data
- Side-by-side source vs derived view renders for sample repo
- Tour steps render and advance via UI control
- Fallback path tested when WASM load fails
acceptance_criteria: Playground passes fetched ref data to WASM, renders derived view side by side with source, exposes 3+ tour steps, falls back gracefully on WASM failure. Tests cover happy + WASM-failure paths.

title: Expand authored docs beyond stub pages
description:
site/src/routes/docs currently has three near-empty stubs (getting-started, concepts, playground). Spec calls for install/quickstart, conceptual guides for the Git-backed model, walkthroughs that connect repo data to Sideshowdb concepts, examples, tutorials, and links into the generated reference. Current pages do not meet that bar.

EARS:
- The Sideshowdb docs section shall provide a Getting Started page with installable Zig 0.16 prerequisites, build commands, and at least one verifiable end-to-end example.
- The Sideshowdb docs section shall provide an Architecture page that explains events, refs, projections, and local-first operation as derived views over Git.
- The Sideshowdb docs section shall provide a Projection Model walkthrough mapping a real public repo to Sideshowdb concepts.
- The Sideshowdb docs section shall provide a Concept-to-Reference cross-link for each concept page, pointing to the relevant generated /reference/ symbol.
- Where a docs page describes user-facing behavior, the page shall cite the EARS requirement it documents.

Acceptance:
- Getting Started, Architecture, Concepts, Projection Walkthrough, Playground Guide exist with non-stub content
- Each concept links into /reference/ for at least one anchor symbol
- Sidebar navigation reflects new structure
- bun run check + bun run test pass
acceptance_criteria: Five authored docs pages with real content (getting-started, architecture, concepts, projection walkthrough, playground guide). Each links into /reference/. Sidebar updated. Tests pass.

title: Add Zig doc-comments with runnable examples across src/core
description:
Autodoc reference page is sparse because public symbols in src/core/*.zig lack /// doc-comments and example blocks. Authoritative low-level reference must include intent, parameters, errors, and at least one example per public symbol so the generated /reference/ page meets a usable docs bar.

EARS:
- The Sideshowdb reference docs shall provide a documentation comment for every public declaration in src/core/*.zig.
- Where a public function can fail, the reference docs shall enumerate each error variant with a plain-language explanation.
- Where a public function performs non-trivial work, the reference docs shall include at least one runnable code example exercised by an existing test.
- If a public declaration is intentionally undocumented (internal-only re-export), then the source shall mark it with an explicit rationale comment instead of leaving it bare.

Acceptance:
- zig build docs (or scripts/build-zig-docs.sh) produces /reference/ with no public symbol missing a doc-comment
- Examples in doc-comments are referenced by, or copied verbatim from, real test code so they cannot rot silently
- Lint script (added by this issue) fails CI when an undocumented public decl is added
acceptance_criteria: zig build docs produces reference page where every public src/core/*.zig declaration has doc-comment + error enumeration + at least one example. CI lint blocks undocumented public decls.
close_reason: Implemented in PR #7 (squash merge). Every pub decl under src/core has /// doc-comment + error enumeration + example references. checkCoreDocs lint gate wired into CI.

title: Design GitHub Pages site for docs, product, and playground
description: Create a design for a GitHub Pages site that combines product marketing, authored documentation, generated Zig reference docs, and a browser-only playground for exploring public GitHub repositories through the sideshowdb model.
design: Use a SveltePress site shell with Graph Atlas visual direction, hybrid documentation model, Zig-generated reference docs, and a browser-only read-only playground for public GitHub repositories.
acceptance_criteria: A written design spec defines the site architecture, information architecture, visual direction, GitHub Pages deployment flow, public-repo-only playground behavior, and testing boundaries.
close_reason: Closed

title: Add release workflow for tag-triggered binary publishing
description:
Add .github/workflows/release.yml that triggers on git tag push (v*) and produces release artifacts: CLI binaries for linux/macos/windows on amd64+arm64 (6 binaries) plus the wasm32-freestanding artifact. Cross-compile from a single ubuntu-latest runner using zig. Publish all artifacts to a GitHub Release.

Acceptance:
- Workflow triggers on tags matching v*
- Produces 6 CLI archives (linux-amd64, linux-arm64, macos-amd64, macos-arm64, windows-amd64, windows-arm64)
- Produces sideshowdb.wasm
- Creates GitHub release attached with all artifacts
- Uses Zig 0.16.0 to match CI
close_reason: Release workflow merged in #5 and validated by tag v0.0.1-alpha01: 6 CLI archives + wasm + SHA256SUMS published; ubi install verified end-to-end on macos-arm64.

title: Fix PR CI and split workflow jobs
description: Bring the CI workflow split and caching improvements onto the document PR branch, fix formatting drift reported by GitHub Actions, and push until PR #3 is green.
close_reason: Closed

title: Implement namespaced versioned document read/write slice
description: Implement the first public document slice over Git refs: add a namespaced, versioned DocumentStore over RefStore, expose doc put/get via the CLI and host-backed WASM surface, add EARS requirements, and cover latest/versioned retrieval with tests.
close_reason: Closed

title: MVP: git ref storage abstraction + impl
description: Implement first concept from spec — controlled-section storage backed by a git ref. Add a RefStore trait-style abstraction (zig vtable pattern modeled on std.mem.Allocator) plus a GitRefStore implementation that drives git plumbing (hash-object, mktree/write-tree, commit-tree, update-ref) via subprocess. Ship a spec doc explaining the storage model and embedding a zig-trait-vs-Scala-3-trait primer for the project author. Tests run against a real ephemeral git repo.
design: Ref namespace refs/sideshowdb/<section>; tree under ref holds blobs keyed by path. Writes use a private temp index file so we never touch the working index. Subprocess-based for MVP — libgit2/zig-native parsing is later work.
acceptance_criteria: Spec doc lands at docs/development/specs/git-ref-storage-spec.md; RefStore + GitRefStore compile; integration test creates temp repo, puts a key, reads it back, lists keys, deletes, and verifies via raw git plumbing; zig build / zig build test green
close_reason: RefStore abstraction + GitRefStore impl shipped. Spec at docs/development/specs/git-ref-storage-spec.md includes Zig-vs-Scala-3 trait primer. 9/9 tests pass including end-to-end git plumbing roundtrip.

title: Bootstrap multi-module zig project skeleton
description: Initial scaffolding for sideshowdb in zig 0.16. Provide core library module, native CLI executable, and wasm32-freestanding library target. No spec implementation yet — only build wiring, module graph, hello-world entrypoints, and tests scaffolding.
design: Single build.zig defines: (1) core module exported via b.addModule; (2) cli exe linking core; (3) wasm static lib with wasm32-freestanding target linking core; (4) test step covering core
acceptance_criteria: zig build succeeds; zig build test passes; zig build wasm produces wasm artifact; zig build run prints sideshowdb banner
close_reason: Multi-module skeleton landed: core lib, native CLI, wasm32-freestanding client, integration tests. zig build / test / wasm / run all green.

title: LevelDB write-behind cache backend aligned with RocksDB storage layer
description:
Add LevelDB support as a write-behind local cache backend that occupies the same storage-architecture layer as RocksDB (disposable materialization/index layer, never canonical truth).

Why:
- Some environments benefit from a lightweight embedded KV option with broad ecosystem support and simpler deployment than RocksDB.
- The storage architecture already treats local engines (e.g., RocksDB, IndexedDB) as disposable indexes/materializations over Git-backed canonical state.
- A LevelDB backend gives a middle-ground durability/perf profile for native deployments and expands backend portability.

Scope:
- Introduce a LevelDB-backed cache/index component in the local materialization layer.
- Position it as write-behind relative to canonical Git/ref operations: staged writes are persisted locally first, then flushed/committed to canonical ref storage under defined ordering and failure semantics.
- Define backend selection and configuration surface alongside existing refstore/storage selection patterns.
- Specify crash-recovery and replay semantics so canonical truth remains reconstructible from Git even if local cache is lost/corrupt.
- Add tests for ordering, recovery, and no-loss-of-canonical invariants.
- Document when to choose LevelDB vs RocksDB.

Out of scope:
- Replacing Git as source of truth.
- Remote sync conflict policy changes.

EARS:
- Where LevelDB backend is enabled, the system shall treat LevelDB as a local write-behind materialization/cache layer and not as canonical truth.
- When a write is accepted into the LevelDB write-behind queue, the system shall flush the write to canonical Git/ref storage in deterministic order.
- If the LevelDB cache is deleted or corrupted, then the system shall rebuild local state from canonical Git/ref history without data loss of canonical records.
- If a write-behind flush fails, then the system shall surface a recoverable error state and shall not claim canonical commit success until Git/ref persistence succeeds.
- The system shall provide configuration and documentation that distinguish LevelDB and RocksDB backend trade-offs for native deployments.

Acceptance:
- Integration test: ordered writes flushed from LevelDB queue are reflected in canonical Git/ref history in the same order.
- Recovery test: deleting LevelDB cache and rehydrating reproduces state from canonical history.
- Failure-path test: simulated flush failure does not report canonical success and exposes recoverable status.
- Docs/spec update: storage architecture docs include LevelDB role and LevelDB-vs-RocksDB guidance.

title: RocksDB-backed RefStore for native server scenarios
description:
Add a RocksDB-backed RefStore implementation for embedded/server deployments where Git on-disk format is undesirable but durability and high write throughput matter.

Why:
- Prior design discussions identified RocksDB as a target backend for native sideshowdb running as an embedded library or service.
- ZiggitRefStore (Git on disk) is good for portability and inspection, but not optimal for high write throughput or large key spaces.
- Pluggable RefStore backends are already part of the architecture; adding RocksDB exercises that boundary and unlocks the embedded-server use case.

Scope:
- Wire a Zig-friendly RocksDB build (cgo/zig-cc shim or existing Zig RocksDB binding) gated behind a build option (-Drefstore=rocksdb or similar) so default builds remain dependency-free.
- Implement RocksDBRefStore with the full RefStore vtable; map keys/versions onto column families or composite keys.
- CLI selector: '--refstore rocksdb' joins ziggit and subprocess, with config + env precedence already defined in storage spec.
- Tests: integration-level put/get/list/history/delete; reload across processes; benchmark suite (separate issue if needed).
- Docs: README + storage spec entry covering when to pick RocksDB vs ziggit.

Out of scope: replication (covered by remote sync issue).

Depends on: nothing hard-blocking, but should land after Option A so we ship MVP without a heavy dependency first.

title: WASM RefStore: ziggit-on-virtual-FS (Option B follow-up)
description:
Make ZiggitRefStore runnable inside wasm32-freestanding by abstracting its Platform.fs over a virtual in-memory filesystem.

Why:
- MemoryRefStore (Option A) gives WASM a working RefStore but loses the git-shaped on-disk layout that ziggit produces natively.
- A virtual-FS port lets the same in-process Git RefStore run in browser, preserving objects/refs structure for parity with native and enabling later browser-side push to a real remote.

Scope:
- Refactor ziggit_ref_store.zig so platform_instance is an injectable parameter rather than a file-scope const tied to std.Io.Dir.
- Implement a wasm-friendly platform impl backed by a std.StringHashMap-style virtual FS (paths -> bytes) using std.heap.wasm_allocator.
- Add wasm32-freestanding compile gate that selects the virtual FS platform.
- Tests: native runs ziggit on tmp dir (existing); freestanding test target runs ziggit on virtual FS and exercises put/get/history.

Out of scope: persistence to IndexedDB (covered by separate sync issue).

Depends on Option A landing first.

title: Stop zig js:install from dirtying bun.lock
description: Running Zig-owned JS orchestration steps such as 'zig build js:acceptance' currently invokes 'bun install' in a way that rewrites bun.lock even when dependency contents do not change. This leaves otherwise clean worktrees dirty after verification and forced us to manually restore bun.lock during sideshowdb-28n. Follow up by making the Zig-managed Bun install path reproducible for verification/CI workflows without mutating the tracked lockfile on success.
acceptance_criteria: - Running 'zig build js:acceptance -Doptimize=ReleaseSafe' from a clean checkout does not leave bun.lock modified\n- Zig-owned JS orchestration still installs or validates workspace dependencies successfully in CI\n- The chosen install mode is documented or justified in the relevant build orchestration code
close_reason: Completed after syncing bun.lock for the acceptance workspace; zig build js:acceptance now leaves the branch clean.

title: Expand TypeScript acceptance coverage beyond minimal CLI/WASM lifecycle
description:
Follow up on sideshowdb-28n after the initial acceptance harness lands. Expand the TypeScript Cucumber acceptance layer beyond the first minimal document lifecycle slice so it covers broader public-contract parity and edge cases across the CLI and WASM TypeScript binding surfaces.

EARS:
- When the acceptance suite expands beyond the initial lifecycle slice, the repo shall cover namespace and version-sensitive document scenarios through the public CLI and WASM TypeScript contracts.
- When additional acceptance scenarios are added, the suite shall continue to assert only documented external behaviors rather than internal implementation seams.
- If new public document behaviors are added to the CLI or WASM TypeScript surface, then the acceptance harness shall gain matching parity scenarios before those behaviors are considered fully covered.

Acceptance:
- Acceptance scenarios cover namespace-aware document flows and at least one version-targeted retrieval flow
- CLI and WASM acceptance coverage stays organized under the dedicated TypeScript acceptance workspace
- The expanded suite remains runnable through the Zig-orchestrated acceptance step

title: Add release automation for TypeScript binding packages
description:
Add release and publishing automation for the public TypeScript binding packages introduced under bindings/typescript/. This follow-up should cover package versioning, publishable metadata validation, and CI/release workflow integration for the vanilla and Effect packages without changing build.zig as the top-level orchestrator.

EARS:
- When a TypeScript binding package is prepared for release, the repo shall validate its publishable metadata, exports, and version before release automation proceeds.
- When release automation publishes the TypeScript binding packages, the repo shall publish the vanilla and Effect packages as distinct packages.
- If repo-wide release automation runs JavaScript package publishing, then build.zig shall remain the top-level orchestrator or entrypoint for the publish preparation workflow.

Acceptance:
- Repo contains a tracked workflow or documented automation path for publishing bindings/typescript/sideshowdb-core and bindings/typescript/sideshowdb-effect
- Package metadata validation runs before publish
- The two packages can be versioned and released independently or in a clearly defined coordinated flow
close_reason: Added coordinated npm release automation for @sideshowdb/core and @sideshowdb/effect, including package publish metadata, a zig build js:release-prepare staging step, dry-run publish verification, and a release workflow job that publishes both staged packages with provenance. Verified with bash scripts/verify-js-release.sh plus bun run check and bun run test.

title: Add TypeScript Cucumber acceptance layer for CLI and WASM
description:
Add a repo-wide TypeScript acceptance test layer using Cucumber to exercise Sideshowdb through stable public contracts rather than internal implementation seams. This follow-up should cover both the native CLI and the shipped WASM module boundary, and should become the formal external acceptance harness for the public TypeScript bindings introduced by sideshowdb-3da.

EARS:
- When acceptance scenarios run for the CLI, the acceptance layer shall exercise documented CLI behaviors through command execution and observable stdout/stderr results.
- When acceptance scenarios run for the WASM/module boundary, the acceptance layer shall exercise the shipped WASM artifact through stable public binding contracts rather than raw internal test hooks.
- Where the TypeScript bindings expose document put/get/list/history/delete, the acceptance layer shall be able to assert those behaviors through published package interfaces.
- If a repo-wide build runs acceptance coverage, then build.zig shall remain the top-level orchestrator for invoking the underlying TypeScript Cucumber suite.

Acceptance:
- Repo contains a dedicated TypeScript Cucumber workspace or package wired into the top-level Bun workspace
- Acceptance scenarios cover at least one happy-path and one failure-path for CLI and WASM-backed document flows
- build.zig exposes a named step that runs the acceptance suite
notes: Execution in worktree .worktrees/sideshowdb-28n-ts-acceptance. Tasks 1-3 are complete and reviewed: the acceptance workspace is scaffolded, CLI and WASM slices pass through the standard acceptance runner, CLI temp repos now self-configure git identity, the direct WASM path uses the real public @sideshowdb/core loader, and the acceptance work uncovered then fixed a Node ESM specifier bug in @sideshowdb/core. Next: Task 4 root orchestration, js:acceptance wiring, CI coverage, and final verification.
close_reason: Completed

title: Make direct zig test invocation work for git ref tests
description:
The native-refstore parity task asked for zig test tests/git_ref_store_test.zig, but that command fails today because the sideshowdb import alias is only provided by build.zig and plain zig test cannot resolve the module graph for this file. The parity harness itself passes under zig build test --summary all, so this is tooling/test-wiring follow-up rather than a parity regression.

EARS:
- When a developer runs zig test tests/git_ref_store_test.zig, the git ref store test shall resolve its required module imports without requiring zig build-managed aliases first.
- If a test relies on build-only module aliases, then sideshowdb shall provide a documented direct-test invocation that succeeds from the repo root.

Acceptance:
- A documented or code-based direct invocation for the git ref store test passes from the repo root
- The chosen approach does not duplicate core source just to satisfy test wiring
close_reason: Fixed by making git ref store tests self-sufficient for direct zig test under tests/ module root.

title: Make direct zig test invocation work for git ref tests
description:
The native-refstore parity task asked for , but that command fails today because the  import alias is only provided by  and plain  cannot resolve the module graph for this file. The parity harness itself passes under , so this is tooling/test-wiring follow-up rather than a parity regression.

EARS:
- When a developer runs , the git ref store test shall resolve its required module imports without requiring -managed aliases first.
- If a test relies on build-only module aliases, then sideshowdb shall provide a documented direct-test invocation that succeeds from the repo root.

Acceptance:
- A documented or code-based direct invocation for the git ref store test passes from the repo root
- The chosen approach does not duplicate core source just to satisfy test wiring
close_reason: Duplicate of sideshowdb-ft9; the valid follow-up was completed and this earlier malformed duplicate should not remain open.

title: Rename build steps to colon-separated lowercase (site:dev, check:core-docs)
description:
Build step strings currently mix lowercase singletons (run, test, wasm, site) with camelCase compounds (siteAssets, siteOnly, siteDev, sitePreview, siteReferenceDocs, siteInstall, checkCoreDocs). Zig stdlib precedent and user preference is single-word lowercase. Zig build has no nested subcommand parser, but colon-separated names cleanly mimic the pattern.

Proposed rename:
- siteAssets         -> site:assets
- siteReferenceDocs  -> site:reference
- siteOnly           -> site:build
- siteDev            -> site:dev
- sitePreview        -> site:preview
- siteInstall        -> site:install
- checkCoreDocs      -> check:core-docs

The existing single-word steps (run, test, wasm, site, siteInstall) stay. 'site' remains the default aggregator.

EARS:
- Where the build graph exposes a compound step, the step shall use colon-separated lowercase names (e.g. site:dev) instead of camelCase.
- When the rename lands, the assemble-pages-artifact.sh / verify-pages-artifact.sh / CI workflows shall be updated to invoke the new names.

Acceptance:
- All compound build steps use colon-separated lowercase.
- CI (.github/workflows/ci.yml + pages.yml) invokes new names.
- Local docs (CLAUDE.md, README, etc.) reference new names where mentioned.
- 'zig build' help output shows the new naming.
acceptance_criteria: All compound build steps use colon-separated lowercase. CI and docs updated. zig build help reflects new names.
close_reason: Renamed compound build steps to colon-separated lowercase. Updated build.zig, ci.yml, getting-started docs, and ears spec. Verified via zig build --list-steps and successful check:core-docs + wasm builds.

title: Expand WASM browser bridge beyond runtime metadata
description: The current browser wrapper only reads banner/version metadata from the shipped Zig WASM artifact. Follow up with a safer host bridge for document get/put or projection reads, including an explicit request-buffer strategy and clearer error signaling.
close_reason: Completed

title: Reduce GitHub Pages site chunk size
description: The site build currently emits a >500 kB client chunk warning from Vite/SveltePress. Investigate chunk splitting, route-level code loading, or manual chunking so the first-release Pages site has a healthier client bundle.

title: Split CI format and link checks into separate jobs
description: Adjust the GitHub Actions CI workflow so formatting and link validation run in their own jobs instead of being bundled into a single job.
close_reason: Completed by PR #3 under follow-up task sideshowdb-kin

title: Extend document slice with list/delete/history operations
description: Follow the initial namespaced, versioned document read/write slice by adding document list, delete, and version-history traversal semantics across DocumentStore, CLI, and WASM.
close_reason: Implemented document list/delete/history across store, transport, CLI, and WASM with tests

title: Evaluate ziggit backend and host integration for document refs
description: Prototype or evaluate a non-subprocess document ref backend using ziggit and tighten the WASM host integration contract for version-aware latest reads and writes.
close_reason: Completed ziggit native RefStore prototype with parity coverage, downstream regression verification, and documented viability findings; broader WASM browser bridge expansion remains tracked in sideshowdb-3da.

title: WASM RefStore: WASI preview1 target with browser FS shim (Option C follow-up)
description:
Investigate switching the browser WASM build to wasm32-wasi (preview1) so ZiggitRefStore can run unchanged against a host-provided FS shim.

Why:
- Avoids forking ziggit's Platform.fs.
- Lets us pick existing browser WASI shims (e.g. wasi-virtual-fs) and reuse the native ziggit code path.

Scope (spike):
- Prototype build target wasm32-wasi alongside wasm32-freestanding.
- Pick a browser-side WASI runtime/shim, hook it up in loadSideshowdbClient.
- Compare bundle size and wiring cost vs Option B.
- Decide go/no-go; if go, file follow-up implementation issue.

Depends on Option A; Option B may obviate this.

title: Address PR feedback on VersionId alias
description: Respond to PR #3 feedback by introducing a VersionId alias in the RefStore seam and propagating it through implementations without changing behavior.
close_reason: Closed

title: Deduplicate CLI usage handling
description: Refactor the CLI app to use a shared usage message/helper and ensure malformed argument paths return the same usage failure output.
close_reason: Closed
